TextENGNew: The assembly hall was brightly lit.
When I stepped inside, Towada-san and Miyuki-chan were sitting across from each other.
"How did it go?!"
Towada-san asked with a desperate expression.
changeReason: New translation changes 'a stern look' to 'a desperate expression' - Japanese 切実な表情 means desperate/earnest, not stern.

TextENGNew: I could only shake my head in silence.
"Is that so...?"
"So... you didn't find it...?"
changeReason: New translation changes 'you couldn't find him' to 'you didn't find it' - Japanese なかったんですか is ambiguous but refers to searching for something (the person who disappeared), and the pronoun changes from 'him' to 'it.'

TextENGNew: "Right...@k@vS10/62/410100976. We searched far and wide, but it was pitch black out there so we couldn't see anything.@k@vS10/62/410100977. ...Otobe and I were discussing the possibility that he might've been dragged into the woods..."
"Dragged into the woods...? By who?"
"Well...@k@vS10/62/410100979. I mean... I don't really understand that, myself..."
I trailed off and sat down on the tatami for the time being. ...I didn't know what else to say to her.
changeReason: New translation changes 'The conversation was turning sour' to 'I trailed off' - Japanese 言葉を濁す means to speak evasively/trail off, not that the conversation turned sour.

TextENGNew: "...Takumi-kun is alive. I know it."
"...Of course he is."
Even as I said it, I had no confidence.
changeReason: New translation changes 'I didn't believe it' to 'I had no confidence' - Japanese 自信がない means lacking confidence, not disbelief. Different emotional nuance.

TextENGNew: I gazed at the tatami in front of me and pretended to think about something to avoid making eye contact with Towada-san.
I wasn't sure why... but somehow, I had a feeling Kurosawa was no longer alive. But I realized that now wasn't the best time to say it.
I decided to set it aside for now.
The rain- and mud-caked hem of my pants uncomfortably clung to my legs.
"Towada-san...@k@vS10/41/410200406. We'll search again tomorrow morning.@k@vS10/41/410200407. So..."
"Hold up, we can't do that..."
"If we don't tell the police everything that's happened today, things will get bad."
"Cars have gone missing, phones have been broken, and people have started to disappear...@k@vS10/62/410100984. Given our current situation, are we even sure we'll survive until tomorrow morning...?"
"That may be true...@k@vS10/41/410200410. But even so..."
Otobe began to mumble something.
...I still wasn't sure what he was trying to hide, but this wasn't the time to get into it.
"Anyway, I think we need to notify the police today.@k@vS10/62/410100986. It's an abnormal situation, no matter how you look at it."
"But... there's nothing we can do to contact the police, right...?"

"Actually... I thought of a way just a little while ago.@k@vS10/62/410100988. And in order to do that, I need to ask a favor.@k@vS10/62/410100989. Sonozaki-san... would you lend us your car for a little while?"
"...My car?"
Sonozaki-san, who was listening to the conversation from a short distance away, suddenly raised her head in surprise.

"Right.@k@vS10/62/410100991. If I borrow your car, I should be able to drive straight to the Okinomiya police station and talk to them directly.@k@vS10/62/410100992. I think that's a much better plan than waiting here overnight."
"...So, you have a car, don't you?@k@vS10/62/410100994. ...There's no way you walked all the way here through the mountains, dressed in a kimono, right?"
"Ryuunosuke-san, that's not the way to ask for something..."
That last sentence was essentially my way of asking, "You're not a ghost, right?!"
I'm well aware that I won't get a straight answer if I ask that directly...
"...Hehe."
Sonozaki-san may have laughed, but her face looked dead serious.
She stood there without moving a muscle, and the sword still hanging in front of her chest.
Wh... wh-why is she reacting like this...?
Maybe she really isn't a human being... but a ghost?!
"So...@k@vS10/62/410100996. Where did you park your car?"
"On the road in front of the shrine.@k@vS10/43/410400252. Near the base of the stone steps.@k@vS10/43/410400253. ...On the side closer to the village."
Otobe turned to me as soon as he heard that. ...And then he whispered something so quiet that only I could hear it.
"Arakawa-san...@k@vS10/41/410200413. Doesn't that... sound a little weird to you...?"
"What is it, Otobe?"
"We did all that walking earlier, but...@k@vS10/41/410200415. there were no cars anywhere to be seen, right?"
"...That's right."
"...I parked it somewhere out of sight.@k@vS10/43/410400255. In a place that ordinary people would never notice it."
Sonozaki-san fixated her threatening eyes straight toward mine.
That moment, I felt a chill crawl up my spine.
Impossible thoughts began taking concrete shapes inside my head.
Sonozaki-san... didn't actually drive a car here, did she...?
Mion Sonozaki was supposed to be dead. But if the person standing before me was actually a ghost inhabiting this village, that explained everything.
If she was a ghost... it wouldn't be unusual for her to be walking around this village.
In other words... Sonozaki-san... didn't come from outside the village, so she didn't need a car...
"What is it...?@k@vS10/43/410400257. Something odd about what I said?"
Something odd...?
What are you saying...?
There are so many odd things about you...!
My palms were drenched with sweat and my throat was painfully dry. Though I had more to say, my throat felt so strained I couldn't use it.
My nervous system was shattered by the extreme tension.
"Can I... ask one more thing?"
"Go ahead."
"You......@k@vS10/62/410101002. really did come here from elsewhere, didn't you?"
"Hehehehe...@k@vS10/43/410400260. Arakawa... do you think I'm some kind of ghost or demon?"
I couldn't respond.
It was clearly just as she said.
If she was a ghost, she shouldn't have any legs and wouldn't make sounds while walking. But none of those indicators were present.
I quietly stared at Sonozaki-san while she stood there motionless.
No matter how hard I looked, I couldn't discern anything about her true identity. But... there was nothing else I could do.
"Ryuunosuke-san... what's wrong?@k@vS10/40/410300630. You look like you're thinking really hard about something..."
I heard Miyuki-chan's anxious voice from somewhere far away.
I see. I guess it really shows on my face that I've been lost in thought.
My throbbing heart gradually returned to normal.
For a moment, I was at a loss for words. But I boldly spoke up anyway.
"About Sonozaki-san's car......@k@vS10/62/410101005. Are we sure she really has one?"
"Haah~..."
"What an idiotic thing to say.@k@vS10/43/410400263. How would I have gotten here if not in a car?"
"Are you seriously going to suggest that I walked several kilometers down mountain roads dressed in a kimono and sandals just to come to this deserted village?"
I didn't think anything of the sort.
However... what if she was "in" this village from the very start...?
That's right. Sonozaki-san was here the whole time.
So in order to disguise that, she had to "refuse" the request I made earlier...
Letting me borrow her car wasn't an unreasonable request.
...The idea that Sonozaki-san was a ghost repeated over and over inside my head, and it was getting stronger.
Then, what should I do now...?
Borrowing Sonozaki-san's car was no longer an option. I had to come up with another plan... In the end, was walking the only option...?
Down a mountain road with no streetlights, on a night like this...?
"...Fine.@k@vS10/43/410400266. I just have to lend you the car, right?"
"Huh?"
Sonozaki-san abruptly rose to her feet as she said that, then began carefully walking forward.
Her car... really... exists...?
......No, that can't be...!
Restlessness consumed my heart as I watched her walk off.
Given the current situation, there was a distinct possibility Sonozaki-san would never return here ever again.
Was she trying escape somewhere on her own and leave us behind...?!
Or was she keeping us confined here, intent on picking us off one by one...?!
"Sonozaki-san...!"
"You're... coming back, aren't you?@k@vS10/62/410101009. Once you get your car, you're not going to leave us like this... right?"
"...My, you're awfully distrusting.@k@vS10/43/410400269. If you're that worried, you can tag along."
"...How about it? Want to come with me?"
I was completely baffled by Sonozaki-san's offer. ...Unbelievable. I never expected she'd ask me to come along.
So... what on earth am I going to do...?!
Feeling lost, I turned back toward Miyuki-chan.
She looked dazed for a moment, but then her eyes widened and she nodded to me.
"...I'll wait here."
"Towada-san, I think it would be best for you to rest here a little longer.@k@vS10/40/410300633. ...Don't you think?"
"Really, it's fine.@k@vS10/40/410300635. Let's stay here together.@k@vS10/40/410300636. ...The two of us can wait here until Ryuunosuke-san and the others come back for us."
"...Okay."
Towada-san appeared vexed by Miyuki-chan's suggestion, but she nodded in agreement.
"Oh... that's right."
What about Otobe?
I stole a quick glance at Otobe as I pondered that.
Otobe was irritatingly indecisive, but the fact that I instinctively wanted to know others' opinions might have shown I wasn't all that great at making decisions either.
Sure enough, Otobe was looking back and forth between myself and Sonozaki-san, at a total loss for what to do.
"Hurry up and decide before the sun comes up.@k@vS10/43/410400272. I'm going on ahead... you guys do what you want."
Sonozaki-san slipped on her sandals, popped open her old-fashioned umbrella, and started walking toward the shrine gate in the pouring rain.
"Sonozaki-san...!"
Aah, I need to make a decision...!
I called out to Sonozaki-san from behind.
